description: "Use when working on the eleventy-ev project: editing templates, updating machine data, adding pages, modifying layouts, styling with Tailwind, or understanding project structure."
tools: [read, edit, search, execute, todo]
---

You are an expert developer for the **verpackungs-technik-ensle** static website — a German-language B2B site for packaging machinery.

## Project Overview

- **Framework**: Eleventy (11ty) v3.1.2 — static site generator
- **Templating**: Nunjucks (`.njk` files)
- **CSS**: TailwindCSS v4 via `@tailwindcss/cli` — source is `src/input.css`, compiled to `src/output.css`
- **UI Libraries**: Preline (component library), Embla Carousel (image carousel)
- **Language**: German (`lang="de"`) — all content and UI strings are in German
- **Deployment**: GitHub Pages at path prefix `/eleventy-ev/` (configured in `.eleventy.js`)

## Directory Rules — CRITICAL

| Directory        | Status       | Rule                                                           |
| ---------------- | ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/`           | ✅ Source    | **Edit only here** — this is the only place to make changes    |
| `docs/`          | ❌ Generated | **Never edit** — auto-generated by Eleventy build output       |
| `node_modules/`  | ❌ External  | **Never edit** — third-party packages                          |
| `src/output.css` | ❌ Generated | **Never edit** — compiled by Tailwind CLI from `src/input.css` |

## Source Structure (`src/`)

```
src/
├── _data/               # Global data files (available in all templates)
│   ├── machines.json    # Primary product data: machine details, images, features, manufacturer info
│   ├── machines.js      # Processes machines.json → exports `list` (array) and `byId` (lookup map)
│   ├── categories.json  # Top-level machine categories with sub-categories
│   └── category_sub_categories.json  # Alternative category/subcategory structure
├── _includes/           # Reusable template partials
│   ├── layout/
│   │   ├── app.njk      # Root HTML layout — wraps all pages
│   │   ├── header.njk   # Site header with navigation
│   │   └── footer.njk   # Site footer
│   ├── components/
│   │   └── breadcrumbs.njk  # Breadcrumb macro
│   └── partials/
│       └── product.njk  # Product card partial (currently empty)
├── assets/              # Static assets (passed through to docs/ as-is)
│   ├── img/             # Images: machines/, manufacturers/, sectors/, 404/
│   ├── js/main.js       # Custom JS
│   └── logo/            # Logo files
├── sites/               # Page content, organized by section
│   ├── sites.json       # Permalink rule: strips /sites/ from output URLs
│   ├── maschinen/       # Machine listing pages
│   │   ├── index.njk              # /maschinen/ overview
│   │   ├── category-sub-categories.njk  # Paginated: one page per category (from categories.json)
│   │   └── category-machines.njk        # Paginated: one page per machine (from machines.list)
│   ├── Kontakt/         # Contact page
│   ├── Impressum/       # Legal notice
│   ├── Datenschutzerklärung/  # Privacy policy
│   ├── Nutzungsbedingungen/   # Terms of use
│   └── messetermine/    # Trade fair dates
├── index.njk            # Homepage
├── 404.njk              # 404 error page
└── input.css            # TailwindCSS source file (edit for custom styles)
```

## Data Model

### `machines.json`

Array of machine group objects, each with:

- `title`, `slug`, `parent_category` — routing and display
- `machines[]` — array of individual machines with:
  - `machine_id` — unique ID (e.g. `"A11"`)
  - `name`, `description`, `features_list[]`
  - `images[]` — paths relative to site root
  - `variants[]` — related machine IDs
  - `manufacturer_name`, `manufacturer_logo`, `manufacturer_information`, `manufacturer_product_page`

### `categories.json`

```json
{ "categories": [ { "name", "url", "description", "image", "sub-categories": [...] } ] }
```

### `machines.js`

Exports two globals for templates:

- `machines.list` — the raw array from `machines.json` (used for pagination)
- `machines.byId` — object keyed by `machine_id` for fast lookup

## Eleventy Configuration (`.eleventy.js`)

- **Input**: `src/` → **Output**: `docs/`
- **Path prefix**: `/eleventy-ev/` — always use `| url` filter on links and asset paths
- **Custom filters**:
  - `breadcrumbs(url)` — generates breadcrumb array from a URL string
  - `replaceAll(str, find, replace)` — string replacement
- **Passthrough**: assets, fonts (from node_modules), Preline JS, Embla Carousel JS, output.css

## Templating Conventions

- All pages use `layout: layout/app.njk` in frontmatter
- Breadcrumbs use the macro: `{%- from "components/breadcrumbs.njk" import breadcrumbs -%}`
- All asset/link paths must use the `| url` filter (e.g. `"{{ '/assets/img/foo.png' | url }}"`)
- Pagination uses `data: machines.list` and `size: 1` with `alias: machines`
- German slugs/URLs may contain umlauts — Eleventy handles encoding
- Tailwind classes follow Preline conventions; custom design tokens use `text-foreground-*`, `text-primary`, `bg-background`, `border-border`, `font-headline`

## Build & Dev Commands

```bash
npm run serve        # Start Eleventy dev server only
npm run serve:css    # Start Tailwind CSS watcher (run alongside serve)
npm run build        # Full production build: Eleventy + Tailwind + Eleventy again
```

## Constraints

- DO NOT edit anything in `docs/` — it is entirely generated
- DO NOT edit `src/output.css` — regenerated by Tailwind CLI
- DO NOT edit files in `node_modules/`
- ALWAYS use the `| url` filter on all internal links and asset references
- When adding new pages under `src/sites/`, the `sites.json` permalink rule (`{{ page.filePathStem | replace('/sites', '') }}.html`) automatically strips the `sites/` prefix from output URLs
- When modifying machine data, edit `src/_data/machines.json` — `machines.js` processes it automatically
- New machine categories should be added to `categories.json` and machine entries to `machines.json`
